issue_title: React: Cloud IDE - 5-30 min open times, npm install/submission slow, white screen on preview, 500/502 errors, submission failures, test case failures post routers, import failures, package.json issues
rca_description: Learning Platform: Latency Issues: Identified latency is caused by pulling the IDE Docker image. The current image size is 1.5 GB, resulting in slow image download times during startup. Remote Server Issues: Rajesh X Evaluation Process Issues: 1. NodeJS deprecated methods are being used to test - util undefined. 2. If a user adds new packages to package.json, but the evaluation is performed using the default package.json that does not include those user-added dependencies, the application will encounter import errors because the required packages are not installed in the evaluation environment. Content Design/Development: No Specific Instructons about Precautions with IDE
solutioning_direction: One reliability program: provision and load-test every student-facing service at full-cohort concurrency (8,000 IDE instances target); slim images and pre-warm pools; fix evaluation pipeline defects; graceful error handling for AWS/pnpm/internal errors; per-campus health monitoring; resolve third-party rate limits (Kaggle shared IPs).

rca_group_category: Release Quality, Rollout Audits & Issue Feedback Loop
issue_title: All Courses: Answer explanations missing across practice, quizzes, and exams; 'Show Explanation' feature unavailable
rca_description: Feature regression plus content gap. The 'Show Explanation' feature broke in a release roughly two months ago and survived because no learning-experience regression checklist or 'explanation shown' telemetry exists. Separately, some assessment items were authored without explanation fields at all, so even after the platform fix those items will still have nothing to surface. Both the platform gate and the content authoring definition-of-done need the fix.
solutioning_direction: One quality program: learning-experience release regression checklist; rollout completeness audits per course/campus; telemetry on submission success, explanation-shown, and score distributions; issue triage queue with SLAs and reporter feedback; sync quiz unlocks to campus delivery progress.

issue_title: Students completing quizzes and MCQ practice using Comet
rca_description: Assessment integrity model predates agentic AI browsers. Quizzes are unproctored client-side experiences with no AI-assistance detection, so completion data is now unreliable as a learning signal.
solutioning_direction: Redesign assessment integrity for the agentic era: AI-assistance detection or proctoring strategy, assessment formats that stay valid with AI present, cross-browser control coverage.

issue_title: Copy/paste of code snippets possible via Safari
rca_description: Copy/paste restrictions implemented with browser-specific APIs not covered on Safari. Integrity controls weren't cross-browser tested.
solutioning_direction: Redesign assessment integrity for the agentic era: AI-assistance detection or proctoring strategy, assessment formats that stay valid with AI present, cross-browser control coverage.

issue_title: Curriculum Design: Spillover teaching eats practice time; no practice after each concept
rca_description: Session content is authored at >1hr of delivery for a 1hr slot, so teaching spills into practice time by design. Pedagogy spec (explain-show-practice per concept) exists but content volume makes it impossible for instructors to follow.
solutioning_direction: Enforce a delivery-time budget (content <= 75% of slot) as a content QA gate; re-author offending sessions to explain-show-practice; rebalance LLM Apps for live coding; publish a theory-vs-hands-on session map so expectations are set correctly.

solutioning_direction: Enforce a delivery-time budget (content <= 75% of slot) as a content QA gate; re-author offending sessions to explain-show-practice; rebalance LLM Apps for live coding; publish a theory-vs-hands-on session map so expectations are set correctly.

rca_group_category: Semester Re-baseline, Scheduling Rules & Practice Protection
issue_title: Curriculum Design: Sem 2 has DS&Algo + React + DBMS simultaneously - too much to comprehend
rca_description: Three heavy, unrelated tracks were packed into one semester that is effectively ~4 months after exams. BOS credit requirements forced the load; the academic plan absorbed it by parallelizing instead of sequencing, causing constant context switching.
solutioning_direction: Re-baseline every semester plan to the post-exam teaching window; protected practice ratio that cannot be rescheduled away; difficulty-weighted hour allocation; hard scheduling rules: no two lectures back-to-back, protected GenAI slots, exam prep buffers, co-curricular/sports slots; quiz adherence tracking.

solutioning_direction: Re-baseline every semester plan to the post-exam teaching window; protected practice ratio that cannot be rescheduled away; difficulty-weighted hour allocation; hard scheduling rules: no two lectures back-to-back, protected GenAI slots, exam prep buffers, co-curricular/sports slots; quiz adherence tracking.

solutioning_direction: Re-baseline every semester plan to the post-exam teaching window; protected practice ratio that cannot be rescheduled away; difficulty-weighted hour allocation; hard scheduling rules: no two lectures back-to-back, protected GenAI slots, exam prep buffers, co-curricular/sports slots; quiz adherence tracking.

issue_title: Observability: Session-practice rhythm broken in sem 2; 75% of practice hours cut

solutioning_direction: Re-baseline every semester plan to the post-exam teaching window; protected practice ratio that cannot be rescheduled away; difficulty-weighted hour allocation; hard scheduling rules: no two lectures back-to-back, protected GenAI slots, exam prep buffers, co-curricular/sports slots; quiz adherence tracking.

rca_group_category: Practice Item Bank Overhaul & Adaptive Engine
issue_title: All Courses: Want personalized practice targeting weak topics
rca_description: Platform has performance data but no adaptive practice engine. Item banks are not tagged at the granularity (topic x difficulty) needed to drive personalization, so the data can't be acted on.
solutioning_direction: One bank-and-engine program: shared difficulty/application taxonomy across practice banks and GRIT; author scenario/story items (SQL, Python); strip in-prompt hints and timer-gate hint access; adaptive practice engine with difficulty/count controls and weak-topic personalization; gamified formats (SQL Murder Mystery style); aptitude bank refresh.

solutioning_direction: One bank-and-engine program: shared difficulty/application taxonomy across practice banks and GRIT; author scenario/story items (SQL, Python); strip in-prompt hints and timer-gate hint access; adaptive practice engine with difficulty/count controls and weak-topic personalization; gamified formats (SQL Murder Mystery style); aptitude bank refresh.

issue_title: All Courses: Some topics PPT-only with no videos; PPTs buffer/fail; module changed mid-topic
rca_description: Content production pipeline shipped topics before video assets were complete, with no completeness gate. Separately, PPT rendering/CDN performance is not monitored. Mid-topic module changes indicate uncontrolled content updates pushed to live cohorts.
solutioning_direction: Content definition-of-done gate (videos + practice + explanations + setup walkthroughs) before any unit goes live; Market Intelligence & Gap Analyser driving a refresh pipeline for version changes; explanation and analogy authoring standards in QA; backfill MongoDB, normalization, and reference materials; proactive version-change comms to students.

issue_title: English: Real-time sessions not implemented in class
rca_description: Real-time activities are in the design but delivery isn't tracked or assessed (see related item below), so instructors skip them with no visibility or consequence.
solutioning_direction: Redesign around scenario/debate/improv session formats with small-group speaking structures; build activity tracking and assessment for real-time sessions so delivery is enforced; fix item time budgets; adaptive practice; presentation-skills track; publish NextTalk cadence.

issue_title: English: 100-word quiz question - time insufficient, everyone stops attempting
rca_description: Assessment item demands writing volume that exceeds quiz time budget. Item authoring has no time-per-item validation, and once students learn it's unscoreable-in-time, rational abandonment follows.
solutioning_direction: Redesign around scenario/debate/improv session formats with small-group speaking structures; build activity tracking and assessment for real-time sessions so delivery is enforced; fix item time budgets; adaptive practice; presentation-skills track; publish NextTalk cadence.

issue_title: English: Want debates and scenario-based sessions; 35-40% unconfident on B1; large classes limit speaking practice
rca_description: Three stacked causes: (1) English curriculum is lecture-and-MCQ shaped, not communicative-practice shaped; (2) class sizes of 40-70+ make per-student speaking time near zero; (3) students rationally deprioritise English because technical tracks carry the visible stakes. Confidence only builds with repeated speaking reps the current design can't supply.
solutioning_direction: Redesign around scenario/debate/improv session formats with small-group speaking structures; build activity tracking and assessment for real-time sessions so delivery is enforced; fix item time budgets; adaptive practice; presentation-skills track; publish NextTalk cadence.

issue_title: English: Practices boring; can be made adaptive
rca_description: Practice banks are repetitive static MCQs. Same adaptivity and item-variety gap as programming practice.
solutioning_direction: Redesign around scenario/debate/improv session formats with small-group speaking structures; build activity tracking and assessment for real-time sessions so delivery is enforced; fix item time budgets; adaptive practice; presentation-skills track; publish NextTalk cadence.

issue_title: English: Want presentation mentorship and practice platforms
rca_description: Presentation skills aren't a defined curriculum outcome with sessions and rubrics; they're assumed to emerge. No structured practice venue exists.
solutioning_direction: Redesign around scenario/debate/improv session formats with small-group speaking structures; build activity tracking and assessment for real-time sessions so delivery is enforced; fix item time budgets; adaptive practice; presentation-skills track; publish NextTalk cadence.

issue_title: English: Real-time sessions have no activity tracking or assessment
rca_description: Real-time activities were added to the curriculum without a corresponding tracking entity in the platform. What isn't tracked isn't delivered (see non-implementation issue above) - this is the upstream cause.
solutioning_direction: Redesign around scenario/debate/improv session formats with small-group speaking structures; build activity tracking and assessment for real-time sessions so delivery is enforced; fix item time budgets; adaptive practice; presentation-skills track; publish NextTalk cadence.

issue_title: English: Question on NextTalk practice frequency per week
rca_description: Session cadence isn't published to students; communication gap between academic planning and student-facing schedule, not a design defect.
solutioning_direction: Redesign around scenario/debate/improv session formats with small-group speaking structures; build activity tracking and assessment for real-time sessions so delivery is enforced; fix item time budgets; adaptive practice; presentation-skills track; publish NextTalk cadence.

issue_title: Curriculum Design: Curriculum not aligned to event tech stacks; master classes out of sync
rca_description: Master class calendar and core curriculum calendar are owned by different teams with no sync checkpoint. Events assume prerequisites the curriculum hasn't delivered yet at that point in time.
solutioning_direction: Lead-authored framework: curriculum-to-masterclass mapping; semester-wise roadmap with foundational/intermediate/advanced mix; readiness package per class (prerequisites, glossary, outcomes, instructor discussion guides); delivered-topics registry; upfront objective/difficulty comms; feedback-and-engagement-driven optimization.

issue_title: GenAI Master Classes: Same intro sessions repeated, wasting slots
rca_description: No central registry tracks which master class topics each cohort has already received. Speakers/topics are booked per event without checking delivery history.
solutioning_direction: Lead-authored framework: curriculum-to-masterclass mapping; semester-wise roadmap with foundational/intermediate/advanced mix; readiness package per class (prerequisites, glossary, outcomes, instructor discussion guides); delivered-topics registry; upfront objective/difficulty comms; feedback-and-engagement-driven optimization.

issue_title: Master Classes: Too low-level vs subject content
rca_description: The masterclasses were intentionally designed to provide industry exposure, awareness of emerging technologies, and beginner-friendly introductions to industry-grade skills through sessions delivered by practitioners from leading technology companies. However, the selected topics and depth of coverage were not sufficiently aligned with the students' ongoing curriculum, creating a mismatch between learner expectations and the intended objective of the masterclass.
solutioning_direction: Lead-authored framework: curriculum-to-masterclass mapping; semester-wise roadmap with foundational/intermediate/advanced mix; readiness package per class (prerequisites, glossary, outcomes, instructor discussion guides); delivered-topics registry; upfront objective/difficulty comms; feedback-and-engagement-driven optimization.

issue_title: Master Classes: New terminology, fast high-standard delivery unlike regular sessions
rca_description: Masterclasses are designed as industry exposure sessions delivered by external practitioners working on cutting-edge technologies. Students reported difficulty understanding unfamiliar terminology and concepts due to the advanced nature of the topics and the pace of industry-oriented discussions.
solutioning_direction: Lead-authored framework: curriculum-to-masterclass mapping; semester-wise roadmap with foundational/intermediate/advanced mix; readiness package per class (prerequisites, glossary, outcomes, instructor discussion guides); delivered-topics registry; upfront objective/difficulty comms; feedback-and-engagement-driven optimization.

solutioning_direction: Lead-authored framework: curriculum-to-masterclass mapping; semester-wise roadmap with foundational/intermediate/advanced mix; readiness package per class (prerequisites, glossary, outcomes, instructor discussion guides); delivered-topics registry; upfront objective/difficulty comms; feedback-and-engagement-driven optimization.

issue_title: Master Classes: Kaggle assignment takes ~1 week to evaluate
rca_description: The assessment workflow for the initial masterclasses relied on manual evaluation of approximately 2,000 notebook submissions by the content team. The evaluation process was not designed to scale with the number of submissions, resulting in review backlogs and turnaround times of up to one week.
solutioning_direction: Lead-authored direction: migrate manual assignments to competition-based automated scoring; standardize masterclass assessment on automated platforms; long-term, build a Kaggle-like ecosystem (automated evaluation, leaderboards, feedback generation, observability) scoped together with Launchpad integration and GitHub profile push.

issue_title: New Feature: LeetCode-style competitive platform with points and leaderboard

solutioning_direction: Lead-authored direction: migrate manual assignments to competition-based automated scoring; standardize masterclass assessment on automated platforms; long-term, build a Kaggle-like ecosystem (automated evaluation, leaderboards, feedback generation, observability) scoped together with Launchpad integration and GitHub profile push.

issue_title: React IDE: Copy/reuse code between questions; side-by-side question preview
rca_description: IDE workspace is scoped to a single question with no cross-question access or side-by-side reference panel. IDE UX designed for isolated assessment, not learning workflows where reuse is legitimate.

issue_title: Curriculum Design: Mandatory subjects allocated maximum timings on portal
rca_description: BOS/AICTE-mandated subjects carry fixed hour requirements. The plan allocated them maximum time by default rather than negotiating minimum-compliant hours, squeezing skill courses.
solutioning_direction: Per-university negotiation agenda: minimum-compliant AICTE hour allocation; explicit decision on portal coverage for university-delivered subjects; college ID issuance to unlock education tiers for coding-agent tools; credit weight for aptitude.

issue_title: GenAI: Coding agents introduced but no sessions given (paid tokens blocked)
rca_description: Paid Tokens & Credits Unavailability All of these tools need paid tokens to run Ideal solution: If student has college mail id, they can get free subscriptions, and use them temporarily MRV students dont have college ids issued.
solutioning_direction: Per-university negotiation agenda: minimum-compliant AICTE hour allocation; explicit decision on portal coverage for university-delivered subjects; college ID issuance to unlock education tiers for coding-agent tools; credit weight for aptitude.

issue_title: Aptitude: Not taken seriously because it carries no marks
rca_description: Assessment policy assigns aptitude zero credit weight. Students optimize for marks; an unweighted track is rationally ignored. Incentive design problem, not content problem.
solutioning_direction: Per-university negotiation agenda: minimum-compliant AICTE hour allocation; explicit decision on portal coverage for university-delivered subjects; college ID issuance to unlock education tiers for coding-agent tools; credit weight for aptitude.

issue_title: University Subjects: Want portal content for AICTE subjects; 3D modelling sessions; instructor support
rca_description: University-delivered AICTE subjects sit outside NxtWave's content scope by the partnership agreement. Students experience one portal but two content owners; the scope boundary was never communicated, and no decision exists on whether to extend portal coverage.
